feedback_text: The mentorship I received was absolutely outstanding and transformed my professional skills.
I learned more in these 3 months than in my entire academic career so far.
My supervisor was always available and gave constructive feedback every single week.
The projects assigned were real and impactful which made the experience incredibly rewarding.
I felt like a genuine team member and not just an intern fetching coffee.
The company culture is warm welcoming and truly supports learning and growth.
I improved my Python and machine learning skills tremendously during this internship.
The structured onboarding process made me feel comfortable from day one.
Working on live client projects gave me exposure I could never get in university.
I would 100 percent recommend this internship program to every student I know.
The tasks were completely unrelated to my field of study and felt like a waste of time.
I received zero guidance from my supervisor for the first two weeks.
The communication from management was extremely poor and confusing throughout.
I felt ignored and undervalued during my entire time at this organization.
The stipend was insultingly low compared to the workload they expected.
There was no structured learning plan and I had to figure everything out alone.
My feedback and suggestions were never acknowledged or even considered.
The internship was chaotic unorganized and left me more confused than when I started.
I was given tasks that any random person could do with no skill development at all.
This has honestly been the worst professional experience of my career so far.
The experience was average nothing particularly exciting but nothing terrible either.
Some days were productive and others felt like I was just filling time.
The internship met my basic expectations but did not exceed them in any way.
Work was okay but the environment could have been more engaging and stimulating.
I learned a few new skills but the pace was slower than I would have preferred.
The team was decent though collaboration could have been better organized.
Overall a standard internship experience with room for significant improvement.
The projects were fine but lacked the depth I was hoping for as a learning opportunity.
Neither impressed nor disappointed with this internship if I am being completely honest.
It was a decent start to my career but I expected more mentorship and direction.

My technical skills grew beyond what I imagined possible in just three months.

I published my first data dashboard that is now used by the actual sales team.
The internship gave me real confidence to pursue a full-time career in tech.
Best decision I ever made was joining this internship program this semester.
I was trusted with responsibilities that even junior full-time employees did not have.
The skills I built here directly helped me ace three job interviews afterwards.
The amount of support and encouragement from seniors was truly heartwarming.
I left every day feeling proud of what I accomplished which says a lot.
Nobody explained what the goals of the internship were even after I asked multiple times.
I spent most of my time doing data entry which had nothing to do with my major.
The supervisor was dismissive and made me feel stupid for asking basic questions.
I was promised a certificate and recommendation letter but neither was delivered.
The workplace was toxic with a lot of internal politics that affected interns too.
I struggled to stay motivated because the work had no clear purpose or direction.

I regret choosing this internship over the other offer I had at the time.

The internship felt like free labor with no real investment in our development.
The work was manageable and I got to try out a few tools I had not used before.
My supervisor was okay but our one-on-one meetings could have been more frequent.
The internship program had good intentions but execution was inconsistent.
Some projects were interesting but others dragged on without clear deliverables.
I gained moderate experience but expected a more challenging and engaging workload.
The team was friendly enough but did not go out of their way to include interns.
The stipend was fair and the work-life balance was better than I expected honestly.
There were moments of genuine learning but also long stretches of idle time.
The experience was neither transformative nor harmful just comfortably average.
I appreciated the flexibility but sometimes lacked the structure needed to grow.
This internship completely changed how I see data and its power to drive decisions.
Shoutout to my amazing mentor who always had time to answer my endless questions.
I came in knowing basic Excel and left knowing Python SQL and Tableau.
The Friday knowledge sharing sessions were my absolute favorite part of the week.
I have three job offers right now and all the credit goes to what I learned here.
The internship felt like a real job and that professional exposure was priceless.
Got my first ever LinkedIn recommendation from a senior data scientist today.
The team pushed me to present my work to actual stakeholders which was terrifying but amazing.
I built three portfolio projects during this internship that interviewers love asking about.
My confidence in technical interviews skyrocketed after this internship experience.

The lack of feedback made it impossible to know if I was doing well or poorly.
I would strongly warn any student against applying to this internship program.
The internship was decent and the team was professional if a bit distant at times.
I appreciated having some real responsibilities even if the scope was a bit limited.
The experience was okay though the onboarding could have been much better organized.
My manager was fairly helpful but the overall program lacked a clear structure.
The work was repetitive at times but the people I met made it worthwhile.
I would consider applying for a full-time role but I am not fully convinced yet.
Some weeks were fantastic and others were slow but that is probably normal.

Reasonable experience for a first internship though more advanced interns may find it lacking.
I learned some things but wished there was more opportunity for creative thinking.
The hands-on machine learning project I completed here is now my top portfolio piece.
I was genuinely sad on my last day because I had grown so attached to the team.

This program made me realize that data science is exactly the career path I want.
I cannot thank the organizers enough for creating such a meaningful experience.
The internship had no performance review process so I never knew where I stood.
I was never introduced to other departments which made the experience very narrow.
The projects had no clear deadlines which made it hard to manage my time well.
I was given conflicting instructions by two supervisors and it caused a lot of stress.
The intern portal was broken for the first month and nobody fixed it promptly.
There was no exit interview or feedback session at the end which felt dismissive.
The internship ended abruptly without any closing ceremony or acknowledgment.
I had hoped to build a network but left without a single meaningful connection.
The tools we were given access to were outdated and made the work unnecessarily hard.
The management team clearly had no plan for how to effectively utilize interns.
I finished each day knowing exactly what I had accomplished and that felt great.
The team trusted me with data that actually influenced a business decision made.
My supervisor wrote me a glowing LinkedIn recommendation without me even asking.
The exposure to agile methodology and sprints was something I could not get elsewhere.
I felt like a professional from week one and that mindset shift was everything.
Working alongside senior data engineers taught me more than any course ever could.
The company invested in a Coursera subscription for all interns which was thoughtful.
I loved the Friday retrospectives where we openly discussed what worked and what did not.
The transparency of leadership made me feel safe to ask questions and make mistakes.
I am leaving this internship with a job offer and it is the best outcome I could ask for.
